narratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically American) foreign policy and perception, portraying it as hysterical and unjustly antagonistic towards Russia and China, which aligns with the narrative of discrediting Western diplomatic stances and actions.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narratives.reasoning: The statement blames Western bureaucrats for catastrophic outcomes and portrays Western governance as deeply flawed and responsible for negative consequences, fitting the narrative of highlighting adverse outcomes for Western countries.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This quote explicitly questions the reliability and motives of Western media, accusing it of misleading framing that has harmful real-world consequences, which is the core of the 'Distrust towards Media' narrative.

narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narratives.reasoning: This statement evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear war, presenting it as an almost inevitable or desirable conclusion due to the current state of affairs, which aligns with amplifying fears about severe war-related consequences.

narratives.evidence_quote: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
narratives.reasoning: The title of the content makes a definitive claim about who is winning the war ('Russia is Winning') and speculates on the meaning and seriousness of the nuclear threat, which fits the narrative of predicting or making assumptions about conflict results.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses the West (specifically America) of consistently and disproportionately exaggerating threats from Russia and China, which aligns with the definition of presenting the West's reactions as disproportionate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that the West, through its media, is deliberately prolonging the war in Ukraine (causing more death) for its own inscrutable reasons, suggesting its actions are not for Ukraine's benefit but for other interests.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations
subnarratives.reasoning: The statement suggests that the Western media's framing actively hinders and prolongs negotiations, implying that diplomatic efforts are being undermined and are ineffective.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing …
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote argues that Western (specifically American) demonization and aggressive posturing towards Russia is an overreaction that creates unnecessary conflict, implying that the West's confrontational policies are misguided and counterproductive, which aligns with the idea that Western actions (like sanctions or political pressure) will have negative, self-inflicted consequences.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons, framing a 'full nuke war' as a potential, albeit grim, outcome to end the conflict, which aligns with the subnarrative definition.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: I am sure Scott will have a safe place he can go .. he talks the most sense ….pity he cannot all but talk about it….most of the rest of the “un Nuclear war countries” will have to survive the horrors left behind

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Ukrainian army is collapsing
subnarratives.reasoning: The title directly states that Russia is winning, which implies the opposing force, the Ukrainian army, is losing or collapsing, aligning with the subnarrative definition of suggesting a significant decline in the Ukrainian military's effectiveness or position.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going? Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote speculates on the potential future actions (resorting to nuclear weapons) and motivations (desperation) of a party in the conflict, which is a speculative statement about war outcomes. It does not fit the specific definitions about army collapse or loss of territory, so it is classified as 'Other' under the parent narrative.
